title: When Faced with a Big Event: - Ask Yourself:
subtitle: Question Big Causes for Big Events
description:
When faced with a big event:
- Ask yourself: "Is there solid evidence for a big cause, or am I assuming it because the event is large?"
- Break it down: Look at smaller, logical factors that might explain the event.
- Challenge conspiracy thinking: Focus on verified sources and avoid overgeneralizing.

Example: A major market crash? Analyze economic trends rather than assuming a single catastrophic cause.
keywords: Question Big Causes for Big Events

howTo.title: Gather immediate evidence
howTo.summary: Spend 10 minutes collecting information from primary and secondary sources. Focus on verified data to avoid assumptions based on the event's size.

howTo.title: Break down the event
howTo.summary: Identify 3 to 7 smaller, plausible causes for the event. Assign rough percentage weights to each cause to reflect their likelihood.

howTo.title: Choose a proportionate response
howTo.summary: Decide on actions based on the weighted causes. Ensure that your response matches the expected impact and is reversible if needed.

howTo.title: Reflect on the process
howTo.summary: After the event, review your decisions and outcomes. Analyze how well your initial weights matched the actual causes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and improve your decision-making skills. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/proportionality-bias-big-events/.
